claim: HappyTeams is a bootstrapped startup founded in Dublin, Ireland.
Michael Dever is the founder and CTO of HappyTeams.
HappyTeams launched its app on Heroku Postgres and after one year experienced scaling issues.
In early 2022 HappyTeams migrated its database from Heroku Postgres to Supabase in less than an hour using pg_dump and pg_restore.
The migration to Supabase reduced HappyTeams' costs.
Supabase provided faster and more consistent performance than Heroku Postgres for HappyTeams.
Supabase supports a large number of direct connections and connection pooling with PgBouncer, solving HappyTeams' connection limit issues.
HappyTeams added a comments feature in two days using Supabase.
HappyTeams plans to expand its usage of Supabase by exploring Edge Functions and the API client.
Supabase is described as "the Postgres development platform" offering a full Postgres database for each project.
Supabase provides built-in authentication with Row Level Security (RLS).
Supabase offers Edge Functions that allow custom code execution without managing servers.
Supabase includes a Storage product for storing and serving large files such as videos and images.
Supabase provides Realtime subscriptions for real‑time data synchronization.
Supabase supports Vector embeddings and integrates with OpenAI and Hugging Face for AI/ML workloads.
The Supabase homepage displays the number "104.9K", indicating a metric such as community size or sign‑ups.
Supabase claims to be "Trusted by fast‑growing companies worldwide" and lists customer stories such as Maergo and Chatbase.
Supabase offers ready‑to‑use RESTful APIs generated instantly from the database schema.
Supabase provides a template library to start new projects in seconds.
Supabase saved over a million dollars by eliminating Meta Workplace
Supabase saved another million dollars by replacing vendor websites
eXp Realty saved more than $3 million annually across multiple systems using Supabase
eXp Realty has 70+ vibe-coded applications in production built on Supabase
Supabase has created over 44,000,000 databases
Supabase has over 200,000 users
Supabase provides SOC 2 Type II and HIPAA compliance
Supabase offers read replicas and high‑availability architecture for enterprise workloads
Supabase Edge Functions are serverless functions powered by Deno and deployed globally
Supabase includes the pgvector extension for AI/ML applications enabling fast semantic search and embedding storage
Supabase automatically generates REST and GraphQL APIs for every table in the database
Supabase is described as "just Postgres" and offers a fully managed PostgreSQL database
Supabase supports multi‑region deployment with optional read replicas for global availability
Supabase provides point‑in‑time recovery and daily automatic backups
Supabase provides a RESTful API using PostgREST, a thin API layer on top of Postgres.
The Data REST API endpoint URL follows the pattern https://<project_ref>.supabase.co/rest/v1/.
The API is instant and auto‑generated: updates to the database schema are immediately reflected in the API.
Supabase generates self‑documenting API documentation in the Dashboard that updates with database changes.
The API is secured with Postgres Row Level Security and is provisioned behind an API gateway with key‑auth enabled.
Supabase benchmarks show basic reads are more than 300% faster than Firebase.
The REST API can serve thousands of simultaneous requests and works well for serverless workloads.
The API supports basic CRUD operations, arbitrarily deep relationships among tables/views, and works with Postgres views, materialized views, foreign tables, and functions.
The REST API resolves all requests to a single SQL statement, providing fast response times and high throughput.
Supabase provides a full Postgres database for every project with Realtime functionality, database backups, extensions, and more.
Supabase Auth allows adding and managing email/password, passwordless, OAuth, and mobile logins through a suite of identity providers and APIs.
Supabase Storage lets users store, organize, transform, and serve large files integrated with Postgres and Row Level Security policies.
Supabase Realtime enables listening to database changes, syncing user states across clients, and broadcasting data to subscribed channels.
Supabase Edge Functions are globally distributed server‑side functions that execute code close to users for low latency.
Supabase offers client libraries for JavaScript, Flutter, Python, C#, Swift, and Kotlin.
Supabase provides a REST API and a GraphQL API for accessing data.
Supabase includes a CLI tool for developing, managing, and deploying projects.
Supabase Management API allows managing Supabase projects and organizations.
Supabase integrates with partners such as Amazon RDS, Auth0, Firebase Auth, Firebase Storage, Firestore, Heroku, MSSQL, MySQL, Neon Postgres, Render, and Vercel Postgres.
Supabase offers self‑hosting guides for users who want to host the platform themselves.
Supabase Inc holds the copyright for the documentation.

Supabase offers a Free plan at $0 per month that includes unlimited API requests, 50,000 monthly active users, 500 MB database size, 5 GB egress, 5 GB cached egress, 1 GB file storage, shared CPU, 500 MB RAM, and community support.
Free projects on Supabase are paused after 1 week of inactivity and are limited to 2 active projects.
Supabase Pro plan costs $25 per month for the first project, with additional projects starting at $10 per month.

Pro plan includes 100,000 monthly active users included, then $0.00325 per additional MAU; 8 GB disk size per project included, then $0.125 per GB; 250 GB egress included, then $0.09 per GB; 250 GB cached egress included, then $0.03 per GB; 100 GB file storage included, then $0.0213 per GB.
Pro plan provides email support, daily backups stored for 7 days, 7‑day log retention, and optional log drains at $60 per drain per project.
Pro plan adds team features such as SSO, control over backups, and industry certifications.

Supabase Enterprise plan is for large‑scale applications, offers custom pricing, a designated support manager, uptime SLAs, BYO cloud, 24×7×365 premium enterprise support, private Slack channel, and custom security questionnaires.
All paid plans (Pro and Enterprise) include $10 per month in compute credits, enough to cover one Micro compute instance.
Supabase Micro compute size costs $10 per month, provides a 2‑core ARM CPU, 1 GB RAM, 60 direct connections and 200 pooler connections.
Supabase Small compute size costs $15 per month, provides a 2‑core ARM CPU, 2 GB RAM, 90 direct connections (recommended) and 400 pooler connections (recommended).
Supabase Medium compute size costs $60 per month, provides a 2‑core ARM CPU, 4 GB RAM, 120 direct connections (recommended) and 600 pooler connections (recommended).
Supabase Large compute size costs $110 per month, provides a 2‑core ARM CPU, 8 GB RAM, 160 direct connections (recommended) and 800 pooler connections (recommended), and is included in the Pro plan.
Supabase XL compute size costs $210 per month, provides a 4‑core ARM CPU, 16 GB RAM, 240 direct connections (recommended) and 1,000 pooler connections (recommended), and is included in the Pro plan.

Supabase 2XL compute size costs $410 per month, provides an 8‑core ARM CPU, 32 GB RAM, 380 direct connections (recommended) and 1,500 pooler connections (recommended), and is included in the Pro plan.

Supabase 4XL compute size costs $960 per month, provides a 16‑core ARM CPU, 64 GB RAM, 480 direct connections (recommended) and 3,000 pooler connections (recommended), and is included in the Pro plan.

Supabase 8XL compute size costs $1,870 per month, provides a 32‑core ARM CPU, 128 GB RAM, 490 direct connections (recommended) and 6,000 pooler connections (recommended), and is included in the Pro plan.

Supabase 12XL compute size costs $2,800 per month, provides a 48‑core ARM CPU, 192 GB RAM, 500 direct connections (recommended) and 9,000 pooler connections (recommended), and is included in the Pro plan.

Supabase 16XL compute size costs $3,730 per month, provides a 64‑core ARM CPU, 256 GB RAM, 500 direct connections (recommended) and 12,000 pooler connections (recommended), and is included in the Pro plan.
Supabase's main repository has 104,991 stars and 12,901 forks as of June 29, 2026
Supabase's primary language across its top repositories includes TypeScript, Elixir, Python, and Rust
The supabase/realtime repository has 7,591 stars, 445 forks, and was last pushed on 2026-06-28
The supabase/supabase-js repository has 4,491 stars, 679 forks, and was last pushed on 2026-06-29
The supabase/pg_graphql repository has 3,344 stars, 134 forks, and was last pushed on 2026-06-03
The supabase/mcp repository has 2,771 stars, 372 forks, and was last pushed on 2026-06-10
The supabase/supabase-py repository has 2,533 stars, 499 forks, and was last pushed on 2026-06-26
Supabase's main repository has 1,096 open issues as of its last push on 2026-06-29

verdict.bull: Successful migration to Supabase with reported cost reduction and rapid feature development (comments in 2 days).
verdict.bear: Solo founder, bootstrapped, no market or revenue data, high dependency on a single vendor.

risk_matrix.reason: Only the founder/CTO is mentioned; no other team members, indicating potential key-person risk.
Bootstrapped with no disclosed funding or revenue, making sustainability and growth capacity uncertain.
No market size, competition, or traction data provided, making market risk impossible to assess.
Heavy reliance on Supabase for core infrastructure; vendor lock-in risk exists despite current benefits.

section_summaries.overview: HappyTeams is a bootstrapped Dublin-based startup founded by Michael Dever, currently using Supabase and planning to adopt more features like Edge Functions.
section_summaries.engineering: HappyTeams migrated from Heroku Postgres to Supabase in under an hour, gaining faster performance and solving connection limits, and quickly built a comments feature in two days.
section_summaries.market: The migration to Supabase reportedly reduced HappyTeams' costs, but no broader market data is provided.
